source.title: Calypso Casino first-look map tour (roof to basement walkthrough)
source.notes: NOT a structured attack setup guide — a casual first-look walkthrough (spawns on the roof, breaks soft walls with a hammer/shotgun while narrating layout and room names) recorded before the creator had played real rounds on it. A proper setup-guide video with attack strategies was planned for later. Very little breach-plan, drone-path, or operator-recommendation content exists as a result — most of what's extractable is layout/connectivity, external breach locations, rappel points, and drone vent locations. Only Sledge is mentioned (the operator the creator is running for the tour, not a strategic recommendation), so operatorRecommendations is sparse/empty by design, not omission. Room names are the creator's own best-guess callouts, several of which he's unsure of or contradicts mid-video (e.g. 'gambling' vs 'betting', 'humidor' vs 'cigar shop'). Site boundaries are inferred from the Defense-side companion video (Calypso_Defense_qf3AthSTIDQ.txt), since this attack tour does not clearly declare bomb sites itself. Rewrite pass: could not obtain reliable compass-orientation grounding for this map (official Ubisoft blueprint PNGs contain no room labels or north indicator, and no third-party callout map with confirmed orientation exists yet for this newly-released map) — locations below are left as room/feature names only, without compass prefixes, per spec rule 8.

hatches.note: Small square connector room off the aquarium area has a hatch.

gadgetPlacements.note: Attacker spawn point on the roof pool side; sub-roof level above the main roof.
Several rappel-down points, including one straight into Main Lobby and one reachable only via a rappel-swift move to a hard-to-reach breach.
Soft wall/window looks into the back of the Tequila Bar.

gadgetPlacements.location: Jukebox and Speakers Near South Mezzanine/Main Lobby
gadgetPlacements.note: Shoot out for a sound cue removal.

generalTips.note: Top floor forms a ring with no middle: Barista to Kitchen to Humidor/Cigar to Fish Stairs and back around. No direct cut across the top of Main Lobby — go around the outside or through it.
High soft-wall density in the Kitchen/Cigar/Humidor cluster.

hatches.note: Drop point near the server/vault ventilation area.

gadgetPlacements.note: External breach — drone in from outside before committing.
Two breakable soft walls, connecting further into CCTV.
Opens directly to outside the building, with a rappel drop right there.
Leads to an unfinished floor with a ladder that connects back outside — a second external access point into Vault/Server.

generalTips.note: This basement area (Managers, Weapon Cache, CCTV, Server, Vault Checkpoint) has more external breach points than any other part of the map, including at least two full-outside entries.

hatches.note: On the connector between Bathroom and the Blackjack/Poker/Roulette cluster.

gadgetPlacements.note: External soft-wall breach. Does not connect Poker/Blackjack directly — that wall is not breakable. Scout before committing; attackers can be shot rappelling here.
Secret is a dead end with no interior route in — only entry is vaulting the window.
Dead-end-style room with a soft wall leading outside.

generalTips.note: Poker, Blackjack, Roulette, and Betting are the four clustered gambling rooms. Blackjack/Poker is the most likely bomb site pairing, consistent with the companion Defense video.
Blackjack/Poker external breach angle is a notable defender sightline — be cautious rappelling or pushing that side.

hatches.location: Bar Hatch Near Stairs to Managers
hatches.note: Near the bar stairwell leading down toward the basement Managers area.

gadgetPlacements.note: Drone-accessible vent drops a drone down into the map from above — hard to spot, hard for defenders to watch. A Keeper-style barrier can shut it down.
Soft wall connector leading to another stairwell and back around into Bar/Janitor.
Connects back around to the roof rappel-down point, linking all the way up to the roof.

generalTips.note: This side of the map (Bar/Betting/Wine Stock/Janitor/Tequila Stairs) is noticeably bigger than the Blackjack/Poker/Secret/Registration side, with more depth and rotational options.
Tequila Stairs connects up to the Tequila Bar seen from outside near the top-floor Barista window — vertical link between ground floor and top floor.
